_comment: Spontaneous-fission prompt neutron multiplicity (nu_p) for the spent-fuel SF neutron source: yield_per_decay(n) = (SF_branch from Serpent2 _SF/_A) * nu_p. Most entries (src='IAEA-T1') are transcribed verbatim from Table 1 of the IAEA NDS report (see PROVENANCE.md): the 18 trans-actinides of safeguards interest (Th-232..Cf-252), with the IAEA's own calculated specific neutron yield n_yield_n_s_g (n/s/g) used ONLY as a cross-check and sf/total half-lives for reference. Cm-246 and Cm-248 (src='HZ-BNL36467') are ABSENT from the IAEA table; their nu_p is DERIVED (nu_p = sum_k k*P(k)) from the Holden & Zucker (1985) BNL-36467 SF multiplicity distributions vendored in ../llnl_sf_multiplicity/, NOT typed from memory. That derivation is validated: the same sum over the same source's Cm-242/Cm-244 distributions reproduces the IAEA-T1 nu_p (2.540/2.720) exactly. See PROVENANCE.md for the per-source detail and what the Cm-244 cross-check does and does NOT validate (the SF branching ratio, not nu_p itself).

sources.IAEA-T1: IAEA NDS, Simakov/Verpelli/Otsuka, 'Update of the nuclear data for the neutron emissions for actinides of interest in safeguards', Table 1 (nu_p from JEFF-3.1 / Holden 1985). See PROVENANCE.md.
sources.HZ-BNL36467: N. Holden, M. Zucker, 'Neutron Multiplicities for the Transuranium Nuclides', BNL-36467 (1985) -- the same evaluation the IAEA report cites for the curium nu-bars. nu_p = sum_k k*P(k) over the P(k) distribution vendored in ../llnl_sf_multiplicity/sf_multiplicity.json (LLNL UCRL-AR-228518 Table 4). Validated by reproducing the IAEA-T1 Cm-242/Cm-244 nu_p exactly with the same computation.

Cm-246.nu_p_note: Derived nu_p = sum_k k*P(k) = 2.930 from the BNL-36467 distribution; single (authoritative) source -- no independent second evaluation in hand.

Cm-248.nu_p_note: Derived nu_p = sum_k k*P(k) = 3.130 from the BNL-36467 distribution; double-confirmed by the independent Vorobyev (2005) distribution (3.129).
